perturbation.original_text: We and our representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials completely confidential; provided, however, that (i) any of such information may be disclosed to those of our directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "our representatives") who we reasonably determine need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between us and the Company (it being understood that our representatives shall be informed by us of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by us, and shall each agree to treat such information confidentially) and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents in writing prior to any such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: We and our representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials confidential; provided, however, that (i) any of such information may be disclosed to those of our directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "our representatives") who we reasonably determine need to know such information for any purpose (it being understood that our representatives shall not be informed by us of the confidential nature of such information and shall not be directed by us, and shall not agree to treat such information confidentially) and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may be made without the Company's consent.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces a contradiction. Originally, the clause stated that the company's representatives must be informed of the confidential nature of the Evaluation Materials. The modification removes this requirement and also allows the representatives to disclose the information without the Company's consent. This conflicts with the original intention of maintaining confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: We recognize and acknowledge the competitive value of the Evaluation Materials and the damage that could result to the Company if the Evaluation Materials were used or disclosed except as authorized by this Agreement. The term "Evaluation Materials" includes information furnished to us orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection, and regardless of whether such information is specifically identified as "confidential".
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction with the contradicted text. The original text ensures confidentiality of the Evaluation Materials by requiring representatives to be informed of and agree to maintain confidentiality. The changed text removes this requirement and permits disclosure without the Company's consent. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which acknowledges the competitive value of the Evaluation Materials and the damage that could result from unauthorized disclosure. The modified text allows for unauthorized disclosure, making the sections incompatible and thus creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, in the event that a Possible Transaction is not consummated neither we nor our representatives shall use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose. We will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by our representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, in the event that a Possible Transaction is not consummated neither we nor our representatives shall use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose, unless the company agrees to allow its use. We will not be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by our representatives.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement states that if the transaction is not completed, the Evaluation Materials can't be used for any purpose. Also, the company will be responsible for any breach of the agreement. The modified text then states that the company will not be responsible for the breach made by the representatives, thereby creating a contradiction of who's actually responsible for the breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: We and our representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials completely confidential; provided, however, that (i) any of such information may be disclosed to those of our directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "our representatives") who we reasonably determine need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between us and the Company (it being understood that our representatives shall be informed by us of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by us, and shall each agree to treat such information confidentially) and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents in writing prior to any such disclosure. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, in the event that a Possible Transaction is not consummated neither we nor our representatives shall use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose. We will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by our representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the company is responsible for breaches by its representatives. The changed text states the company is not responsible for breaches by its representatives. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict within the document, specifically contradicting the statement in both the original text and the contradicted text that 'We will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by our representatives.' This falls under the definition of inconsistencies leading to in-text contradictions when different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that we or any of our representatives receive a request or are required (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose all or any part of the Evaluation Materials, we or our representatives, as the case may be, agree to (i) immediately notify the Company of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (ii) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request and (iii) assist the Company in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that we or any of our representatives receive a request or are required (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose all or any part of the Evaluation Materials, we or our representatives, as the case may be, agree to (i) immediately notify the Company of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (ii) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request and (iii) assist the Company in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy. However, representatives can fully disclose such information, even without a protective order.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text adds a direct contradiction. Initially, the clause mandates notification, consultation, and assistance in seeking a protective order before disclosure. The appended phrase, "However, representatives can fully disclose such information, even without a protective order," contradicts the previous requirements, undermining the Company's ability to protect its confidential information. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty regarding the protocol for handling legal requests for Evaluation Materials.
perturbation.contradicted_text: We recognize and acknowledge the competitive value of the Evaluation Materials and the damage that could result to the Company if the Evaluation Materials were used or disclosed except as authorized by this Agreement. The term "Evaluation Materials" includes information furnished to us orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection, and regardless of whether such information is specifically identified as "confidential".
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates notification, consultation, and assistance in seeking a protective order before disclosing Evaluation Materials. The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by stating that 'representatives can fully disclose such information, even without a protective order.' This directly undermines the confidentiality protections detailed in the 'Contradicted Text,' which acknowledges the competitive value of the Evaluation Materials and the damage that could result from unauthorized disclosure.

perturbation.original_text: Accordingly, unless required by applicable law, we agree that prior to the execution of a Sale Agreement with respect to the closing of a Possible Transaction, without the prior written consent of the Company, we will not, and we will direct our representatives not to, disclose to any person either the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction between us and the Company or any to the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof unless we are advised by counsel that we are required to make such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Accordingly, unless required by applicable law, we agree that prior to the execution of a Sale Agreement with respect to the closing of a Possible Transaction, with the prior written consent of the Company, we will, and we will direct our representatives to, disclose to any person either the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction between us and the Company or any to the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof unless we are advised by counsel that we are required to make such disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that without the prior written consent of the company, the representatives are not allowed to disclose to any person that discussions or negotiations are taking place. The modified text changes this and states that with prior written consent of the Company, the representatives will disclose to any person that discussions or negotiations are taking place. The contradiction arises because prior to the original statement, the agreement states that they will not disclose any information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: We and our representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials completely confidential; provided, however, that (i) any of such information may be disclosed to those of our directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "our representatives") who we reasonably determine need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between us and the Company (it being understood that our representatives shall be informed by us of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by us, and shall each agree to treat such information confidentially) and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents in writing prior to any such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that unless required by law, the representatives would not disclose any negotiation information without prior written consent. The changed text flips this, stating they will disclose such information with written consent. This directly contradicts the "Contradicted Text" which states that they will keep the evaluation materials completely confidential and specifies that disclosure requires written consent from the Company. The changed text now mandates disclosure with consent, violating the confidentiality previously agreed upon and creating a direct conflict within the document.
